name: Sheftalia (Barbeque Sausages)
recipeIngredient: 500 g Finely ground veal or lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the pork with veal or lamb, onion, parsley, salt, and a generous grinding of black pepper.
2. Dip panna into a bowl of warm water for about 30 seconds, then remove and carefully open out a piece at a time on the work surface.
3. Cut with kitchen scissors into pieces about 10 cm (4 inches) square.
4. Take a good tablespoon of meat mixture and shape into a thick sausage about 5 cm (2 inches) long.
5. Place towards one edge of a piece of panna, fold the end and sides over the meat, and roll up firmly.
6. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
7. Thread sausages onto flat, sword-like skewers, leaving space between them. The number of sausages per skewer depends on their length.
8. Cook over glowing charcoal, turning frequently to ensure even cooking without burning. Do not place too close to the heat as sheftalia must cook slowly so that the inside is well cooked and the outside is nicely browned.
9. The panna melts during cooking, keeping the meat moist and adding flavor. Excessive flare-ups can be controlled by sprinkling water on the coals.
10. Serve sheftalia as an appetizer or main course.
